#+STARTUP: content
* debian reset root password

Reboot the Mac.

When the GRUB menu appears, highlight the Debian entry and press e to edit.

Find the line starting with linux (it usually ends with quiet).

At the very end of that line, add:

#+begin_example
init=/bin/bash
#+end_example

Press F10 to boot.

You will get a root prompt without a password. Run these commands:

Set a simple password (like 'test') that doesn't use symbols

#+begin_src sh
mount -o remount,rw /
passwd root
reboot -f
#+end_src
